explanation: This requirement states that 'prop_0' shall hold at all times when 'prop_1' holds. In LTL, this is typically captured using an implication: globally (if prop_1 then prop_0), i.e., at every point in time, if prop_1 is true then prop_0 must also be true.

The requirement specifies that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold in that state (i.e., the system must provide means to 'prop_0' whenever 'prop_1' is true). This is expressed as: globally, if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also be true.

The requirement states that prop_0 should be provided (i.e., be true/hold) when prop_1 holds. In LTL, this is expressed as: globally, whenever prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true at the same time.

The requirement means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at the same time. In other words, at every point in time, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must hold as well. This can be expressed with a global (G) implication.

The requirement says that while prop_1 is true, the system shall provide means to prop_0. In LTL, this means: as long as prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold. This can be captured by using implication within a global (G) operator: globally, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must also hold.

This requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' holds, the system shall provide 'prop_0'. In terms of LTL, this is expressed as: globally, if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also be true at the same time.

This requirement means that whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 should also hold. In other words, at all times, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true. This is a safety property that can be directly expressed using an implication within the globally operator.

The requirement says that whenever prop_1 holds (i.e., in all states where prop_1 is true), the system shall provide means to prop_0, meaning that prop_0 must also hold in those states. This corresponds to globally (prop_1 implies prop_0).

The requirement states that the system shall provide means to ensure prop_0 occurs while prop_1 holds. This implies that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true (invariant over the entire period that prop_1 holds). This is captured by 'globally, if prop_1 then prop_0'.

The requirement is to enable (provide means to) prop_0, but only during times while prop_1 holds. In LTL, this can be represented using the release operator: as long as prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold. This can be written as G(prop_1 -> prop_0). That is, globally, when prop_1 is true, prop_0 must be true as well.
